reforms.description: The government is pursuing new training projects that emphasize cooperation with industry, aiming to enhance vocational training and improve employment outcomes for graduates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing vocational training improves employment outcomes, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The training projects are actively being pursued.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant but not structural, focusing on vocational training.
reforms.source_quote: The government is at present pursuing new training projects which further emphasise co-operation with industry.

reforms.description: The Mexican government liberalised the foreign investment code, reducing restrictions and facilitating access to technology and management improvements from foreign firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Liberalising foreign investment enhances access to technology and management practices, fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The foreign investment code has been liberalised.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in investment policy.
reforms.source_quote: The government decided to liberalise the foreign investment code beginning in 1984.

reforms.description: The Mexican government reformed the system of standards and metrology to enhance quality control and certification, involving consultation with producers and consumers, and has approved a new law requiring cost-benefit analysis for new standards while promoting the use of private laboratories for quality certification.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving quality control and certification processes supports innovation and market competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms to standards and metrology have been enacted.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but not structural, focusing on quality standards.
reforms.source_quote: In 1988 the Government reformed the Mexican system of standards and metrology.

reforms.description: The 1991 law on intellectual property rights has been enacted, increasing the duration of patent protection and modernizing enforcement mechanisms to combat piracy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening intellectual property rights encourages innovation and investment in new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The law on intellectual property rights has been enacted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of intellectual property law.
reforms.source_quote: The 1991 law on intellectual property rights significantly increased the duration of the protection of patents.

reforms.description: In February 1992, the Mexican Congress approved a major change in Article 27 of the Constitution, ending the land distribution programme and establishing full ownership rights for ejidatarios, allowing for various forms of rural business ventures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing full ownership rights encourages investment and innovation in rural business ventures.
reforms.status_evidence: The constitutional change has been approved by Congress.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in land ownership policy.
reforms.source_quote: In February 1992 the Mexican Congress approved a major change in Article 27 of the Constitution, which implied the end of the land distribution programme initiated in 1917.

reforms.description: Mexico and France signed a Co-operation Agreement to strengthen their political relations, bilateral economic co-operation and promote technological exchange.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the agreement on growth is not clearly defined.
reforms.status_evidence: The agreement has been signed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The agreement is a diplomatic initiative rather than a significant policy reform.
reforms.source_quote: Mexico and France sign a Co-operation Agreement to strengthen their political relations, bilateral economic co-operation and promote technological exchange.
